req.title: Hr-Admin deletes policy-document
req.statement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to delete a policy-document through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers: (1) happy-path deletion by an authenticated hr-admin (EP – valid partition), (2) attempt by an unauthenticated user (NEG/DT – 401 branch), (3) attempt by a user with insufficient role (NEG/DT – 403 branch), (4) deletion with a document-id at the minimum valid boundary (BVA), and (5) state-transition from 'existing' to 'deleted' with audit verification (STC). Five tests are provided to satisfy the mandatory minimum of three and to achieve meaningful coverage across all cited heuristics.

tests.name: test_hr_admin_deletes_policy_document_success
preconditions: At least one policy-document exists in the system
actions: Submit the delete form for the selected document
Observe the HTTP response and UI confirmation
expected: Response body contains the deleted entity id
Policy-document is no longer listed on /policy-documents
An audit record for the deletion is written
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_hr_admin_deletes_policy_document_success(hr_admin_page, create_policy_document, audit_log_client):
    doc_id = create_policy_document(title="EP Test Doc")
    page = hr_admin_page

    page.goto("/policy-documents")
    row = page.locator(f"[data-doc-id='{doc_id}']")
    expect(row).to_be_visible()

    row.locator("[data-action='delete']").click()
    page.locator("[data-testid='confirm-delete']").click()

    response = page.wait_for_response(
        lambda r: "/policy-documents" in r.url and r.request.method in ("DELETE", "POST")
    )
    assert response.status in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {response.status}"

    body = response.json()
    assert str(doc_id) in str(body), "Entity id missing from response"

    page.reload()
    expect(page.locator(f"[data-doc-id='{doc_id}']")).not_to_be_visible()

    audit_entry = audit_log_client.find_latest(entity_id=doc_id, action="delete")
    assert audit_entry is not None, "Audit record not written"

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_delete_policy_document
preconditions: No active session / user is not authenticated
actions: Send DELETE/POST request to /policy-documents without authentication credentials
expected: No audit record is written for this attempt
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_delete_policy_document(base_url, create_policy_document, audit_log_client):
    doc_id = create_policy_document(title="NEG Unauth Doc")

    response = requests.delete(
        f"{base_url}/policy-documents/{doc_id}",
        headers={},
        timeout=10
    )

    assert response.status_code == 401, f"Expected 401, got {response.status_code}"

    verify_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/policy-documents/{doc_id}",
        headers=<admin_auth_headers>,
        timeout=10
    )
    assert verify_resp.status_code == 200, "Document should still exist"

    audit_entry = audit_log_client.find_latest(entity_id=doc_id, action="delete")
    assert audit_entry is None, "No audit record should exist for unauthenticated attempt"

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_cannot_delete_policy_document
preconditions: User is authenticated but has role:employee (not hr-admin)
actions: Authenticate as a user with role:employee
expected: No audit record is written for this attempt
trace: R-WEB-055 – error pathway 403 if role forbidden
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_forbidden_role_cannot_delete_policy_document(base_url, employee_auth_headers, create_policy_document, audit_log_client):
    doc_id = create_policy_document(title="DT Forbidden Role Doc")

    response = requests.delete(
        f"{base_url}/policy-documents/{doc_id}",
        headers=employee_auth_headers,
        timeout=10
    )

    assert response.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got {response.status_code}"

    verify_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/policy-documents/{doc_id}",
        headers=<admin_auth_headers>,
        timeout=10
    )
    assert verify_resp.status_code == 200, "Document should still exist"

    audit_entry = audit_log_client.find_latest(entity_id=doc_id, action="delete")
    assert audit_entry is None, "No audit record should exist for forbidden attempt"

tests.name: test_delete_policy_document_with_minimum_valid_id
preconditions: A policy-document with the minimum valid integer id (e.g. id=1) exists
actions: Locate the document with id=1 (minimum boundary id)
Submit the delete form for that document
Observe the HTTP response and UI state
expected: Response body contains entity id equal to 1
Document with id=1 is removed from the listing
An audit record for the deletion is written
trace: R-WEB-055 – boundary value on entity id field
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

MIN_VALID_ID = 1

def test_delete_policy_document_with_minimum_valid_id(hr_admin_page, ensure_policy_document_exists, audit_log_client):
    ensure_policy_document_exists(doc_id=MIN_VALID_ID, title="BVA Min ID Doc")
    page = hr_admin_page

    page.goto("/policy-documents")
    row = page.locator(f"[data-doc-id='{MIN_VALID_ID}']")
    expect(row).to_be_visible()

    row.locator("[data-action='delete']").click()
    page.locator("[data-testid='confirm-delete']").click()

    response = page.wait_for_response(
        lambda r: "/policy-documents" in r.url and r.request.method in ("DELETE", "POST")
    )
    assert response.status in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {response.status}"

    body = response.json()
    assert str(MIN_VALID_ID) in str(body), "Min boundary entity id missing from response"

    page.reload()
    expect(page.locator(f"[data-doc-id='{MIN_VALID_ID}']")).not_to_be_visible()

    audit_entry = audit_log_client.find_latest(entity_id=MIN_VALID_ID, action="delete")
    assert audit_entry is not None, "Audit record not written for min-id deletion"

tests.name: test_delete_policy_document_state_transition_existing_to_deleted
actions: Verify document is in state=active via GET /policy-documents/{id}
Submit the delete form for the document via the UI
Attempt to GET /policy-documents/{id} again to verify state transition
expected: Before deletion: document state is active/present (200)
After deletion: GET /policy-documents/{id} returns 404 or state=deleted
An audit record capturing the state transition is written
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_delete_policy_document_state_transition_existing_to_deleted(
    hr_admin_page, base_url, create_policy_document, admin_auth_headers, audit_log_client
):
    doc_id = create_policy_document(title="STC State Transition Doc")

    pre_resp = requests.get(f"{base_url}/policy-documents/{doc_id}", headers=admin_auth_headers, timeout=10)
    assert pre_resp.status_code == 200, "Document must exist (active) before deletion"

    page = hr_admin_page
    page.goto("/policy-documents")
    page.locator(f"[data-doc-id='{doc_id}'] [data-action='delete']").click()
    page.locator("[data-testid='confirm-delete']").click()

    del_response = page.wait_for_response(
        lambda r: "/policy-documents" in r.url and r.request.method in ("DELETE", "POST")
    )
    assert del_response.status in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {del_response.status}"

    post_resp = requests.get(f"{base_url}/policy-documents/{doc_id}", headers=admin_auth_headers, timeout=10)
    assert post_resp.status_code in (404, 200), "Post-deletion state must be 404 or deleted"
    if post_resp.status_code == 200:
        assert post_resp.json().get("state") == "deleted", "Document state must be 'deleted'"

    audit_entry = audit_log_client.find_latest(entity_id=doc_id, action="delete")
    assert audit_entry is not None, "Audit record must capture the state transition"
